Broken City (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $19,701,164Details
International Box Office $17,413,813
Worldwide Box Office $37,114,977
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,437,213
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,192,938
Total $10,630,151
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In a broken city rife with injustice, ex-cop Billy Taggart seeks redemption—and revenge—after being double-crossed and then framed by its most powerful figure, the mayor. Billy’s relentless pursuit of justice, matched only by his streetwise toughness, makes him an unstoppable force—and the mayor’s worst nightmare.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,268,908 (42.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.38 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,620 opening theaters/2,622 max. theaters, 3.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $28,982,273

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 18th, 2013 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: January 18th, 2013 (Wide) (Taiwan)
January 18th, 2013 (Wide) (India)
January 18th, 2013 (Wide) (Turkey)
January 25th, 2013 (Wide) (Mexico)
February 6th, 2013 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: April 30th, 2013 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for pervasive language, some sexual content and violence.
(Rating bulletin 2228, 6/20/2012)
Running Time: 108 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prologue
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Addiction, Blackmail, Corrupt Cops, Government Corruption, Hitmen, Infidelity, Political, Private Investigator
Social setting: Political Campaign
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Regency Enterprises, Emmett/Furla Films, Black Bear, New Regency, Closest to the Hole, Leverage, Allen Hughes, Envision Media, 1984 Private Defense Contractors
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
